name: Homestead Bourbon Cake (the Homestead/Bath County,va
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the pecans and raisins in bourbon overnight.
2. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
3. Grease and flour a 9-inch springform pan.
4. In a small bowl, mix together flour, hazelnuts, and cinnamon.
5. In a large mixing bowl, combine eggs, sugar, salt, and vanilla extract. Beat until the egg volume triples (about 10 minutes).
6. Gently fold the flour mixture into the egg mixture. Pour batter into prepared pan.
7. Bake for 20 minutes on the middle rack of the oven. Check doneness after 15 minutes by lightly touching the top; it should spring back.
8. Allow cake to cool in the pan for 2 minutes before turning out onto a wire rack to cool completely.
Frosting:
1. Beat butter until light and creamy.
2. Gradually add powdered sugar, about 1/4 cup at a time, mixing well after each addition.
3. Add milk, a little at a time, beating on low until the frosting reaches desired consistency.
4. Stir in bourbon, pecans, and raisins, then beat again to combine.
5. Frost the cooled cake layers and sides. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.
